sections.paragraph:
with suff. צִדְקִי m.-

(1) in a physical sense, straightness, rightness, i.q. ישֶׁר. Psa 23:3, צֶדֶק מַעְגְּלֵי “straight paths.” Hence in an ethical sense-

(2) rectitude, right, what is right and just (das Recht), what is so, or ought to be so, compare ישֶׁר No. 2, a; Psa 15:2, פֹּעֵל צֶדֶק “one doing what is right,” acting rightly; compare עָשָׂה צֶדֶק Isa 64:4 Psa 45:8 Job 8:3 36:3 , לְפֹעֲלִי אֶתֵּן צֶדֶק “to my Creator I will ascribe rectitude,” i.e. I will vindicate his right. Hence שָׁפַט צֶדֶק to judge the right, i.e. justly, Deu 1:16 16:18 Jer 11:20 מִשְׁפַּט צֶדֶק just judgment, Isa 58:2 מֹאזְנֵי צֶדֶק, אַבְנֵי צֶדֶק just balances, a just weight, Lev 19:36 Job 31:6 Eze 45:10 זִבְחֵי צֶדֶק just sacrifices, i.e. such as are due, rightly offered, Deu 33:19 Psa 4:6 51:21 . With a suff. the right, the just cause of any one, Psa 7:9, “judge me בְּצִדְקִי according to my right;” Psa 18:21, 25 Psa 18:25 Job 6:29, compare Psa 17:1. Often joined are צֶדֶק וּמִשְׁפָּט what is right and just, Psa 89:15 97:2 . Also the right which we speak, i.e. the truth, Psa 52:5 Isa 45:19.

(3) justice, i.q. צְדָקָה, as of a judge, Lev 19:15 of a king, Isa 11:4, Isa 11:5 16:5 32:1 of God, Psa 9:9 35:24 , 28 Psa 35:28 50:6 72:2 96:13 hence righteousness, integrity (see צַדִּיק No. 3 ), Isa 1:21 51:1 , Isa 51:7 59:4 Psa 17:15 Hos 2:21.

(4) liberation, welfare, felicity [?] (as being the reward of virtue, see Isa 32:17). So often when there is in the other member יֶשַׁע, יְשׁוּעָה Isa 41:2 45:8 51:5 Dan 9:24 Psa 132:9 (compare verse 16 Psa 132:16). Used of the servant of God, Isa 42:6, קְרָאתִיךָ בְצֶדֶק “I have called thee with deliverance,” i.e. that thou mayest come with deliverance, mayest bring it (compare Jer 26:15), and used in the same sense of Cyrus, Isa 45:13, אָנֹבִי הַעִירֹותִיהוּ בְצֶדֶק “I raised him up (that he may come) with deliverance;” also i.q. בְּרָכָה Isa 61:3, אֵילִיִ הַצֶּדֶק “terebinths of blessing” (auf denen Gottes Seegen ruht, gefeegnete Terebinthen, as it is said gefeegnetes Land). Very frequently also the word צְדָקָה (which see) is thus used; and this signification has been vainly [?] rejected of late by Moeller (De Authentia Esaiæ, p. 186), and Kleinert (Die Echtheit der Jes. Weissagungen, i.p. 255, seq.). Compare צְדָקָה No. 4.
